task: I’m working on a school robotics thing and need to write a short intro for my part. The group is keeping the final presentation brief, so I want something that clearly says what I handled without turning it into a whole speech.

prefs.short: Wants responses short and efficient, not padded out
prefs.explanation: She seems unlikely to tolerate long-winded explanations when she already knows what she wants. Her practical, results-first style suggests she prefers information delivered without extra filler. If a response drags on, she would probably tune it out.

prefs.short: Prefers blunt answers over soft hedging
prefs.explanation: Her guarded, skeptical attitude points to a preference for clear statements rather than cautious phrasing. She would probably respect help that gets to the point and says what matters. Overly tentative or overly polite wording would likely read as weak or evasive to her.

prefs.short: Likes clean, practical formatting she can scan fast
prefs.explanation: She responds to efficiency, so a structured format that makes the answer easy to use would fit her well. Bullets or checklists would likely work better than dense prose because they reduce wasted attention. She is not looking for style; she is looking for something usable.

prefs.short: Wants collaboration, but on her terms
prefs.explanation: She does not seem to want to be controlled or talked down to, even when she needs help. The best support for her would probably offer options and let her keep the final say. Heavy-handed prescribing would likely trigger resistance.

prefs.short: Can handle pushback if it is firm and justified
prefs.explanation: She is skeptical and does not trust easy agreement, so she may respect a challenge that is direct and concrete. At the same time, she is defensive and status-conscious, so pushback has to be sharp enough to be credible without sounding preachy. Weak or moralizing correction would probably make her dismiss the assistant.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — keep it to 1–2 sentences and name your exact piece.

Template:
**“I worked on [your part], focusing on [main task]. I also helped with [one extra thing or result].”**

Example:
**“I worked on the sensor code, focusing on getting the readings accurate and stable. I also helped test the robot so it would run reliably during the demo.”**

If you want, tell me your exact part and I’ll turn it into a polished short intro.

per_pref.reason: Keeps it concise: "keep it to 1–2 sentences" and provides a short template instead of a long explanation.
Fairly direct and concrete, e.g. "name your exact piece" and the explicit template, though softened by "If you want".
Uses clean, scannable formatting with a brief instruction, a bolded template, and an example.
Offers collaboration without taking over: "If you want, tell me your exact part and I’ll turn it into a polished short intro."
No pushback or disagreement is present, so this preference is not meaningfully engaged.

turns.agent_response:
Got it — here’s a more natural version:

**“I worked on the coding for the robot, mainly building the control sequence and fixing problems when it kept getting stuck. I also helped test it so we could make sure it could finish the course.”**

If you want, I can make it sound:
- more casual
- more formal
- more like a class presentation

per_pref.reason: Very concise and efficient: one short intro plus three brief style options. No filler or long explanation.

Mostly direct and plainspoken, e.g. “I worked on the coding...” and “fixing problems when it kept getting stuck.” Slightly softened by “Got it — here’s a more natural version” and the optional tone choices.
Clean, scannable formatting with a bolded quote and bullet list of options. Easy to use fast.

Offers collaboration and choice: “If you want, I can make it sound:” followed by options, letting the user keep the final say. Minor issue is the assistant still proposes a version first, but it’s not heavy-handed.
The response does not involve disagreement or correction, so pushback tolerance is not meaningfully tested here.
